statement_a: The plaintiff seeks damages for breach of contract and unjust enrichment.
llm_response: Those are two common but legally distinct claims — breach of contract is a legal remedy requiring a valid contract, while unjust enrichment is an equitable claim typically pursued as an alternative when no enforceable contract exists. Courts generally don't allow recovery on both simultaneously, as a valid contract usually precludes an unjust enrichment claim covering the same subject matter. Pleading them in the alternative, however, is a standard litigation strategy when the existence or validity of the contract is in dispute.
llm_response_normalized: Those are two common but legally distinct claims — breach of contract is a legal remedy requiring a valid contract, while unjust enrichment is an equitable claim typically pursued as an alternative when no enforceable contract exists.
guard_explanation: Statements are coherent — no significant contradiction detected.
